---
title: "weekly temperature tracker"
format: html
---
Our project will be a weekly temperature tracker program. The program will record daily temperatures for a week, calculate the average, minimum, and maximum, and generate a graph showing trends, highlighting the highest and lowest temperature days.  

Goals: The goal of our program is to track the weekly high and low temperatures in Oklahoma City on a daily basis and record them in our program. The program will then calculate the average, minimum, and maximum temperatures while also generating a graph showing trends. 

Data Resources: National Weather Service website gives the high and low temperatures every day in Oklahoma City. Apple Weather App also gives temperatures in Oklahoma City. 

Code example using python and positron for one week: 
## Initial Code / Data

```{python}
days = ["Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"]
temps = [86,80,77,84,75,76,79]

avg_temp = sum(temps)/len(temps)
min_temp = min(temps)
max_temp = max(temps)

print("Average:", avg_temp)
print("Min:", min_temp)
print("Max:", max_temp)
```

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.plot(days, temps, marker="o")
plt.title("Weekly Temperature Trends")
plt.xlabel("Day")
plt.ylabel("Temperature (°F)")
plt.show()
```
When we ran the cells for our code it generated a graph plotting the weekly temerature treds with temperature on the y axis and the day on the x axis.

Progress: We took daily temperature data from the month of March and put it into a structured dataset that states the high temperatures for each day of the week. We also used this to calculate the mean high for each day. We used this data to create a line graph showing the temperature trends throughout the week. This line graph helps us observe patterns, how the temperature fluctuates, and any noticeable changes in the temperatures. Next, we want to be able to implement the lows for each day as well as the high temperatures. 

New Code:
```{python}
plt.figure()
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

days = ["Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"]

lows = [42, 42, 44, 51, 58, 59, 65]
highs = [73, 71, 76, 80, 83, 77, 82]

# two lines only
plt.plot(days, highs, marker="o", label="Highs", color="red")
plt.plot(days, lows, marker="o", label="Lows", color="blue")

# combine for min/max
all_temps = highs + lows
all_days = days + days
all_values = highs + lows

min_temp = min(all_temps)
max_temp = max(all_temps)

min_index = all_values.index(min_temp)
max_index = all_values.index(max_temp)

# small highlights
plt.scatter(all_days[min_index], min_temp, s=200, color="black", label="Min Temp")
plt.scatter(all_days[max_index], max_temp, s=200, color="black", label="Max Temp")

plt.title("Weekly Temperatures for 4/6/26-4/12/26 in Oklahoma City")
plt.xlabel("Day")
plt.ylabel("Temperature (°F)")
plt.grid(True)
plt.legend()

plt.show()
```
